fences.receipt: #if os(iOS) || os(tvOS) — Core NSUIView shim (base class of every chart view) exists only under os(iOS) || os(tvOS); surface digest marks visionOS absent.
#if os(iOS) — Core Drops class is guarded by #if os(iOS); os(iOS) is false when compiling for the visionOS SDK, and the surface digest resolves visionOS as absent (gap).

unknowns.name: confettiswiftui
unknowns.why: hapticFeedback parameter is present, but checkout source excludes visionOS from haptic playback via an internal !os(visionOS) guard not visible in the public surface; likely a no-op.

unknowns.name: floatingpanel
unknowns.why: Manifest declares only .iOS(.v12). Digest records no visionOS gaps, but Sources/Extensions.swift uses UIScreen.main unconditionally (UIScreen is unavailable on visionOS), so a visionOS build is doubtful; no guard evidence either way.

unknowns.name: grdb.swift
unknowns.why: README: 'Database queues support in-memory databases' (v7.11.1 README.md line 423)

unknowns.name: Kingfisher
unknowns.why: WebP needs the separate KingfisherWebP coder package — nothing in this package serves it

unknowns.name: popupview
unknowns.why: Public decl present per surface digest, but internal sheet implementation is os(iOS)-gated (os(iOS) is false on native visionOS); no visionOS floor in manifest

unknowns.name: progresshud
unknowns.why: Only .iOS(.v17) declared; SwiftUI-based target might compile for visionOS but nothing in the surface or manifest confirms it.

unknowns.name: progresshud
unknowns.why: Only .iOS(.v17) declared; SwiftUI-based target might compile for visionOS but nothing confirms it.

unknowns.name: realm-swift
unknowns.why: README 'Fully Encrypted' section demonstrates Realm.Configuration(encryptionKey: key) with a 64-byte key ('Data can be encrypted in-flight and at-rest'). README does not name visionOS.

unknowns.name: SDWebImage
unknowns.why: SDWebImage's surface is Objective-C (214 files unparsed in v1) — honestly unverified rather than guessed; manifest floors: iOS 9 / macOS 10.11 era

unknowns.name: sqlite.swift
unknowns.why: SQLCipher product is linked only .when(platforms: applePlatforms, traits: ["SQLCipher"]); visionOS is in applePlatforms, so encryption works when the SQLCipher trait is enabled — conditional, hence unknown.

unknowns.name: swift-opus
unknowns.why: visionOS absent from manifest floors (package predates it)

unknowns.name: swiftui-pull-to-refresh
unknowns.why: Surface parses PRESENT and the referenced UIKit types are generally available on visionOS, but there is no visionOS-specific evidence or build verdict.

unknowns.name: swiftui-pull-to-refresh
unknowns.why: Surface parses PRESENT and the SwiftUI+UIKit types used are generally available on visionOS, so compilation is plausible, but there is no visionOS-specific evidence or build verdict.
